qa_pairs.question: How do I configure my `ansys.lic` file to use an institutional Ansys license server that has already been set up for use on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To configure your `ansys.lic` file for an existing institutional Ansys license server on an Alliance cluster, you need to contact your local Ansys license server administrator. They will provide you with two pieces of information: the configured Ansys flex port (FLEXPORT) number, which is commonly 1055, and the fully qualified hostname (LICSERVER) of the license server. Once you have these values, you simply plug them into the `FILE: ansys.lic` template in your `~/.licenses/ansys.lic` file to specify your license server.

qa_pairs.question: What steps are required to use a local Ansys license server that has never been set up for use with an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To use a local Ansys license server that has never been configured for an Alliance cluster, you'll need to obtain the configured Ansys flex port (FLEXPORT) number, the fully qualified hostname (LICSERVER), and the statically configured vendor port (VENDPORT) from your local Ansys license server administrator. You'll also need confirmation that the `<servername>` (found in the first line of your license file) will resolve to the same IP address as LICSERVER on the cluster. Send the FLEXPORT, LICSERVER, and VENDPORT information to Alliance technical support, mentioning which Alliance cluster you want to use. An Alliance system administrator will then open the outbound cluster firewall. You will be provided with a range of IP addresses, which you must give to your local network administrator to request that the firewall into your local license server be opened to allow Ansys license connection requests to FLEXPORT and VENDPORT across that specified IP range.

qa_pairs.question: How can I verify if my `ansys.lic` file is properly configured and working with my Ansys license server on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer:
To test if your `ansys.lic` file is configured and working properly with your Ansys license server, run the following sequence of commands on the same Alliance cluster where you will be submitting jobs:
```bash
[login-node:~] cd /tmp
[login-node:/tmp] salloc --time=1:0:0 --mem=1000M --account=def-YOURUSERID
[compute-node/tmp] module load StdEnv/2023; module load ansys/2025R2.04
[compute-node:/tmp] $EBROOTANSYS/v$(echo ${EBVERSIONANSYS:2:2}${EBVERSIONANSYS:5:1})/licensingclient/linx64/lmutil lmstat -c $ANSYSLMD_LICENSE_FILE | grep "ansyslmd: UP" 1> /dev/null && echo Success || echo Fail
```
A `Success` output indicates that license checkouts should work when jobs are submitted to the queue, while a `Fail` output indicates a problem with the licensing setup that will likely cause jobs to fail.
